name: btrfs-progs
title: btrfs-progs
summary: Checksumming Copy on Write Filesystem utilities
description:
Checksumming Copy on Write Filesystem utilities
Btrfs is a copy on write filesystem for Linux aimed at implementing
advanced features while focusing on fault tolerance, repair and easy
administration.
.
This package contains utilities (mkfs, fsck) used to work with btrfs
and an utility (btrfs-convert) to make a btrfs filesystem from an ext3.

commands.name: btrfs
commands.description: topics about the BTRFS filesystem (mount options, supported file attributes and other)

arguments.description: if supported by the command, print subcommand output in that format (text, json)
arguments.raw: --format  if supported by the command, print subcommand output in that format (text, json)

arguments.description: print condensed help for all subcommands
arguments.raw: --help  print condensed help for all subcommands

arguments.description: set log level (default, info, verbose, debug, quiet)
arguments.raw: --log  set log level (default, info, verbose, debug, quiet)

commands.name: btrfs-convert
commands.description: convert from ext2/3/4 or reiserfs filesystem to btrfs in-place

commands.name: btrfs-image
commands.description: create/restore an image of the filesystem

commands.name: btrfs-map-logical
commands.description: map btrfs logical extent to physical extent

commands.name: btrfs-select-super
commands.description: overwrite primary superblock with a backup copy

commands.name: btrfsck
commands.description: check or repair a btrfs filesystem

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use btrfs because Checksumming Copy on Write Filesystem utilities.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use btrfs as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `btrfs [global options] <group> [<group>...] <command> [options] [<args>]` as documented upstream for btrfs.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair btrfs with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `btrfs --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
